title: ochna beirensis
intro: Ochna beirensis, also known as the "Mugunghwa" or "Korean national flower," is a deciduous shrub native to Korea and a member of the family Ochnaceae. It has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ochna beirensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Ochna beirensis include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat.
